page: Amazon Web Services c8i.16xlarge is a compute-optimized instance powered by the Nitro hypervisor and dedicated Intel Xeon 6975P-C processors. This x86_64 architecture server features 64 vCPUs across 32 physical cores with a clock speed of 3.9 GHz. It is equipped with 128.0 GB of DDR5 memory operating at 7 GHz, yielding a resource density of 2.0 GB of RAM per core. The processor includes a 480 MB L3 cache and supports advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions for accelerated matrix and vector operations. No local storage or GPUs are bundled with this instance type, and it features a baseline network bandwidth of 30 Gbps.

In benchmark evaluations, the c8i.16xlarge demonstrates top-tier performance, particularly in integer and floating-point operations. It achieves top-10% scores in Geekbench single-core and multi-core tests, as well as Passmark CPU metrics. The hardware excels at software compilation, image processing, and text processing. Memory bandwidth is highly competitive, with read-only cached small block transfers reaching 5103.56 GB/sec and uncached large block reads at 197.26 GB/sec. However, large block memory write speeds represent a relative tradeoff, performing in the middle 50% tier at 82.14 GB/sec.

The instance is highly optimized for high-throughput workloads, delivering top-tier results in Redis SET operations and static web serving. It also shows strong capability in machine learning tasks, processing medium-sized 7B LLM prompts at 306.67 tokens/sec, though text generation for larger 70B models falls into the average tier. Given its compute-heavy profile and high memory speed, the c8i.16xlarge is qualitatively cost-efficient for compute-bound tasks, software build pipelines, high-performance web serving, and database operations that do not require local NVMe storage.
description: Amazon Web Services c8i.16xlarge is an x86_64 compute-optimized instance featuring 64 dedicated vCPUs from an Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor running at 3.9 GHz. It is configured with 128.0 GB of high-speed 7 GHz DDR5 memory, providing 2.0 GB of RAM per core, alongside a 480 MB L3 cache. This instance does not include local storage or GPUs but offers a baseline network bandwidth of 30 Gbps. Benchmarks show top-tier performance in software compilation, image processing, and Redis operations, alongside strong memory read bandwidth. Large block memory writes represent a moderate tradeoff, scoring in the middle tier. The c8i.16xlarge is highly suited for compute-intensive workloads, including high-throughput web serving, software compilation, and medium-scale machine learning inference.

tagline: A high-frequency, compute-optimized virtual server with dedicated Intel Xeon processors and high-speed DDR5 memory for intensive workloads.

128.0 GB of high-speed 7 GHz DDR5 memory with 2.0 GB per core
